scam_claim_pattern: police or CBI or ED can arrest you over video call or Skype or WhatsApp
reality: No Indian law enforcement agency (Police, CBI, ED, etc.) has the legal authority to arrest a person digitally or over a video call. This is a common fraud tactic.
citation_note: Under Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), arrest requires actual physical touch or confinement of the body unless the person submits to physical custody.

scam_claim_pattern: I am calling from CBI or ED office regarding your illegal parcel or bank account
reality: Law enforcement agencies do not call citizens to threaten them with 'internal investigations' or 'secret warrants' over the phone for financial details.
citation_note: Pretending to hold public office or performing acts under color of such office falsely is a criminal offense under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

scam_claim_pattern: Pay a security deposit or compliance fee or fine via UPI to avoid arrest or close the case
reality: Official legal fines or bail bonds are never collected via UPI or private bank transfers during a phone call. Demand for money to 'settle' a criminal case is extortion.
citation_note: Legal proceedings follow a formal summons and court process. Immediate payment via UPI is a hallmark of fraud.

scam_claim_pattern: Do not disconnect or talk to family or inform anyone or you will be arrested
reality: There is no legal concept of 'Digital Confinement'. It is a psychological tactic used by scammers to isolate victims from support.
citation_note: Agencies must follow BNSS protocols which include the right to inform relatives and consult legal counsel upon arrest.

scam_claim_pattern: Sending you a warrant on WhatsApp or email for signature
reality: Warrants and summons are official documents served by police personnel in person or through authorized post. WhatsApp is not an official medium for serving arrest warrants.
citation_note: Falsifying digital documents or using fake government seals is punishable forgery under BNS.
